Empires & Puzzles Baishu Review: The Lunar Rat's Balancing Act

Baishu flips onto the scene with a unique 'steal or smack' mechanic. Is this Lunar Rat a masterful balancer or just a niche circus act? We break down his high-variance skill and why he might be the king of War Equalizer.
Baishu Review: Is This Acrobat Worth The Cheese?
Baishu: The Lunar Rat That Steals Your Buffs (And Your Patience)

Monk who operates on a high-risk, high-reward mechanic that feels a bit like gambling at a casino run by rodents. Released during the Year of the Rat (or simply celebrating it), Baishu brings a unique "balancing" act to the board: he either steals buffs and cleanses ailments, or he gets frustrated and smacks everyone for damage.

While he’s technically a modern hero, his reliance on specific board states makes him a "conditional" superstar. He’s the guy who shines brightly when the stars align (specifically in War Equalizer), but might just stand there looking cute when they don't.

Baishu is depicted as an agile, white rat performing an acrobatic act, possibly walking a tightrope or balancing on a beam. He’s decked out in traditional festive attire with gold coins—a symbol of prosperity that hopefully translates to your raid chest. The art style is vibrant and energetic, capturing the nimble nature of the character. It’s a refreshing change from the usual "angry guy with a big sword" aesthetic, though you might find his expression a bit
focused, like he’s trying to figure out where he left his cheese.

), he is significantly behind the current cutting-edge heroes who are pushing 1260+ base power and 1500+ LB2 power.
Decent, but not earth-shattering for an attacker.
These durability stats are acceptable but leave him vulnerable to the massive hits from modern snipers and AoE monsters.
He isn't "paper thin," but he definitely relies on his
class (resistance to ailments) and his speed to survive, rather than raw bulk.

He distributes it to your team. (Great!)

Strictly utility. He doesn't heal HP, but shifting buffs/ailments is a huge swing in momentum.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

This is Baishu's secret weapon. It completely neuters heroes like
Waterpipe, M&M, or the Christmas family
. Their minions become useless meat shields that can't attack or use special effects (like mana cut). This alone makes him a viable counter-pick even if his stats are aging.
A standard defensive LB2 power. It’s okay, helping him punish AoE hitters, but it doesn't synergize directly with his skill like "Special Boost" or "Mana Boost" would.
Upon release, Baishu was seen as a "big brain" hero. High potential, but confusing to use effectively. He wasn't a meta-defining tank, but a beloved toy for manual play.
His stats have fallen off. Hitting for 200% (the most common scenario) is barely a tickle against modern 3000+ HP heroes. The 400% dream hit is rare outside of specific wars.
He is not a "put on defense and forget" hero. He is a surgical tool for
(where buffs/ailments are wiped, maximizing his damage chance) or as a hard counter to minion-heavy teams.
Baishu is a Monk. He needs to survive to fire, but his main value ceiling comes from that massive 400% hit.
Maximizes the damage when the stars align. If he hits for 400%, you want it to kill.
Even with full defense, he's not a tank. Lean into his offensive potential.
. You bring him to change the game state or nuke the board; make the nuke count.

The Equalizer rule naturally sets up his "no buff/no ailment" damage condition.
Sometimes he does 0 damage and steals a useless buff.
Lagging behind the current meta significantly.
You can't just spam him; you have to read the board.
Undispellable buffs/ailments can block his utility
sometimes his damage logic if not careful.
His absolute best mode. The war rule cleans the board, letting him fire his full 400% damage nuke.
Fast damage is king, and disabling minion meat shields is valuable.
Use him manually to counter specific Minion tanks or when you can control the timing.
Baishu loves a clean board. He pairs well with heroes who don't spam passive buffs, or heroes who can force a reset.

Gazelle removes all buffs/ailments from your team (except her own). If you fire Gazelle first, Baishu's "Ailment Cleanse" check fails (triggering 200% damage). You just need the enemy to have no buffs.
A fast/minion-killing team designed to wipe the enemy before buffs stack up.

buffs (like some Constructs or Cartoons) are bad for him because he can't steal them, and it might block his damage trigger if the game counts "attempted steal" as a failure to "successfully steal" but still recognizes the buff exists.
If he tries to transfer an ailment but the enemy resists, it counts as "not successfully distributed," triggering damage. Wait... that's actually good! He deals damage if he
to distribute. So immunity helps him deal damage!

If you pulled him, congratulations! He is a fun, fast hero. Level him up, but learn to read his skill. Don't be disappointed if he doesn't kill everyone instantly—use him to counter minions and steal key buffs like Counterattack or Regeneration.
He’s a luxury toy. If you play high-level War Equalizer, he’s a top-tier pick for an attack team. Otherwise, he’s likely riding the bench behind newer, more consistent Holy heroes like
. Don't Alpha Aether him unless you really love the rat theme.
"The utility of this hero is severely lacking in value... I think a more interesting mechanic would have been to make this hero essentially Bizarro Snow White."
– A forum user lamenting the RNG nature.
"His best use is equalizer war. He hit very hard after equalization."
– A veteran player spotting his true niche immediately.
"You seriously LB2 heroes before reading the text on them? It does exactly what it says… And yes it´s garbage."
– A savage response to a player disappointed with their impulsive LB2 choice.

Baishu is the epitome of "it depends." On a good day, he’s a Fast speed nuclear bomb that shuts down minions. On a bad day, he’s a fragile acrobat who steals a minor mana buff and dies. He balances on the fine line between "trash" and "god tier," making him one of the most interesting—albeit frustrating—heroes in Empires & Puzzles.
Level for War Equalizer and Minion counter-play. Bench for general defense.
A detailed review of Baishu, the 5-star Holy hero from the Lunar New Year event. We analyze his stats, 'Masterful Balance' skill, and viability in the current meta.
